candidate.title: MetaX C600 accelerator catalog field promotion

local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Existing local C600 captures found and reused: raw/crawler/compute-accelerators-metax-c600/20260627T153520667760Z-www-metax-tech-com-prod-html-259649e029.md and raw/crawler/compute-accelerators-metax-c600/20260628T060653669535Z-www-metax-tech-com-prod-html-259649e029.md. No new external fetch was needed.
local_checks.wiki_search: Existing curated comparison text mentioned MetaX C600 as raw comparison evidence, but no metax-c600 SKU, observation, or resolved-spec row existed before this parent task.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: ai_infra wiki/index.md routes this work to Compute Accelerator Spec Catalog, Compute Accelerator Parameter Comparison, and AI Infra Coverage Map; no dedicated MetaX C600 page was needed.
local_checks.prior_manifest_scan: Prior continuation parent-12 promoted C500, C500X, C550, and C588 only. C600 was explicitly left out and remains a distinct product-page identity key.

duplicate_boundary_checks.result: Hardware layer previously listed MetaX C-series sources through parent-12 but did not record a resolved C600 slice. Parent-13 adds C600 raw evidence and unresolved-boundary notes.
Added one known_source entry for parent-13 MetaX C600 fields without mutating blocked_items or unrelated coverage gaps.
No pre-existing metax-c600 SKU row; added one C600 SKU tied to the existing metax-c600-product-page source.
No pre-existing MetaX C600 observations; added observations only for form_factor, interconnect, and tdp.
No pre-existing MetaX C600 resolved fields; added resolved fields pointing to the new C600 observations only.
Existing metax-c600-product-page source registry entry was reused; the source registry was not modified.

Existing C600 raw comparison row now includes resolved-spec citation and explicit caveats for unsupported memory, compute, ecosystem, benchmark, production-operation, server, topology-scale, and supernode claims.

Catalog coverage text now treats C600 as a bounded structured product row while keeping qualitative memory, compute, application, ecosystem, server, and 128-card supernode wording outside resolved fields.

gap_reason: The local corpus already contained official product-specific MetaX C600 captures with source-visible, schema-supported form factor, interconnect wording, and maximum power fields that had not yet been promoted into the structured accelerator catalog.

schema_boundary.source_value: The C600 page says the product has large high-bandwidth memory and full-link ECC protection, but it does not expose exact memory capacity, memory type, or memory bandwidth.
schema_boundary.boundary: No memory_capacity, memory_type, or memory_bandwidth row is promoted.

schema_boundary.source_value: The C600 page says the product has excellent mixed-precision compute, but exposes no exact FP64, FP32, BF16, FP16, FP8, or INT8 value.
schema_boundary.boundary: No compute peak field is promoted.

schema_boundary.source_value: The C600 page describes MetaXLink interconnect technology, low-latency MetaXLink, expandable MetaXLink-E, and 128-card supernode wording.
schema_boundary.boundary: Only interconnect wording is promoted. No topology-scale, supernode, server, or per-link bandwidth field is inferred.

schema_boundary.source_value: The page says MXMACA is compatible with the mainstream GPU ecosystem and lists model training/inference, data processing, general computing, and generative AI application scenarios.
schema_boundary.boundary: No ecosystem-completion, application-coverage, benchmark, production-operation, service-SLO, training-throughput, or inference-throughput claim is promoted.

schema_boundary.source_value: MetaX servers, workstations, supernodes, N-series, G-series, X-series, and unrelated accelerators appear in page navigation.
schema_boundary.boundary: No non-C600 SKU, observation, or resolved row is created or modified by parent-13.

non_promoted_boundaries: No MetaX C500, C500X, C550, C588, N-series, G-series, X-series, server, workstation, supernode, AMD, Intel, Huawei, Enflame, Moore Threads, Iluvatar, Biren, Kunlunxin, Resnics, Corigine, Yusur, Asterfusion, or unrelated accelerator row was promoted or modified by this task.

No exact compute peak, memory capacity, memory generation/type, memory bandwidth, host-interface generation or lane count, benchmark ranking, DeepSeek/model operation, ecosystem compatibility, production incident/postmortem, service SLO, training throughput, inference throughput, local benchmark baseline, MLCommons submission, legal-compliance completion, server product, topology scale, supernode, or aggregate-system claim was promoted from the C600 page.
No new external source was fetched, and no crawler subscriptions or Domain Channels were changed.
Existing H200, Cambricon, Kunlunxin, Biren, Corigine, Resnics, Yusur, Asterfusion, Iluvatar, and prior MetaX C500/C500X/C550/C588 resolved fields were not intentionally changed.
